naive_cost: Run the full Formal Conjectures repository build (975 local Lean source modules across FormalConjectures and FormalConjecturesForMathlib, plus transitive dependencies) after replacing a duplicate local Sidon definition with the existing project API.
opportunities_considered: Build only the disambiguated numeric module target.
Reuse the pinned manifest-identical dependency cache and existing transitive OLean files.
Use one Lean thread under a fixed 16 GiB cap.
Fail before compilation unless the workspace and checkout candidates are byte-identical and match the refactored candidate hash.
Require a nonempty kernel artifact and hash it after the warning-fatal build.
Independently prove the removed local predicate equivalent to the reused project predicate before rebuilding.
chosen_reductions: One exact module rather than the repository; cached dependencies rather than rehydration; exact byte/hash prefilters; a separately compiled equivalence theorem as the semantic guard.
expected_throughput_gain: Only the named Problem 530 module is requested instead of every project root, but the exact reduction relative to a full build is unmeasured. The prior exact-target log itself reported an 8038-job dependency closure and took 887 seconds; those 8038 jobs must not be described as eliminated.
soundness_basis: The prefilters are exact byte/hash checks. The independent Lean audit proves the old and project predicates extensionally equivalent. Lake invokes the pinned Lean kernel on the exact module with --wfail and requires a nonempty OLean.
remains_uncompressed: The module's transitive imports still load normally. Full repository CI, placement review for the new extremal definitions, and maintainer semantic review remain separate gates.
